findings.finding: Task 1 introduces cache-clear write-from-outside-the-helper but _get_cortex_root_payload's check-then-act idiom (server.py:213-215, 242) lacks any lock; pre-existing race becomes newly exploitable once external clears land.
findings.reviewer_angle: Concurrency safety in S1.3 retry-and-cache mutation under asyncio
findings.evidence_quote: add a per-tool-call retry counter (local variable, not module-global) that on CortexCliMissing clears _CORTEX_ROOT_CACHE and retries once.

findings.finding: Task 3 _retry_on_cli_missing helper risks except OSError footgun given CortexCliMissing(OSError); a careless except OSError silently absorbs PermissionError into retry loop, contradicting Task 4 case (c).
findings.reviewer_angle: Concurrency safety in S1.3 retry-and-cache mutation under asyncio
findings.evidence_quote: extract a small _retry_on_cli_missing(func, *args) helper if it cleans up the call sites, otherwise inline the try/except at each site

findings.finding: Trap find . -name '*.md.bak' -delete unscoped — runs against entire repo tree, silently deletes editor backups unrelated to recipe.
findings.evidence_quote: Trap: trap 'find . -name "*.md.bak" -delete' EXIT INT TERM set near the top of the recipe

findings.finding: Path-rewriting scope restricted to *.md but lifecycle slugs are cited in non-markdown files (bin/cortex-* shell scripts, claude/hooks/*.sh, tests/*.py, plugins/**/server.py); after archive runs those references break with no signal.
findings.evidence_quote: rewrite scope INCLUDES docs/, README.md, SKILL.md citers because the find/grep walks all *.md under repo root with only the four exclusions

findings.finding: Plan asserts slug values must be regex-escaped before substitution but does not specify the escape helper or character set; latent footgun if slug naming evolves to include regex metacharacters.
findings.evidence_quote: Three regex anchored substitutions per archived slug — slug values must be regex-escaped before substitution

findings.finding: Task 16 acceptance (>=100 dir moves + zero .md.bak) satisfiable by recipe-repair commits without a distinct lifecycle-archive full run commit existing; verification framework allows recipe-repair commit to silently substitute for labeled full-run commit.
findings.reviewer_angle: Destructive sample-then-full sequencing under recipe-repair scope creep
findings.evidence_quote: Spec N6.4 acceptance: >=100 directory moves under lifecycle/archive/ compared to pre-run state.

findings.finding: No diagnostic procedure distinguishes recipe-bug from data-bug after a sample-run failure; implementer must classify without guidance, and a data-bug-mistaken-as-recipe-bug bakes the data fault into the repaired recipe.
findings.reviewer_angle: Destructive sample-then-full sequencing under recipe-repair scope creep
findings.evidence_quote: if the sample run surfaces bugs in Tasks 11 or 12, fix the recipe with a clearly-labeled lifecycle-archive recipe repair (post-sample) commit before proceeding to Task 16.

findings.finding: 5-of-124 (~4%) sample against ~1869 cross-reference lines undersized for catching rare citation patterns (refs in fenced code blocks, tables, trailing punctuation breaking word-boundary).
findings.reviewer_angle: Destructive sample-then-full sequencing under recipe-repair scope creep
findings.evidence_quote: Plan-residue noted the cross-reference volume is ~1869 lines, two orders of magnitude larger than the recipe's initial framing assumed.
